meaning: adj.编入索引的,[计]被变址的,[经]与生活指数(或物价指数)挂钩的
examples: A quarter of this vast archive has been indexed and made accessible to researchers.
He uses those as discussion materials and then he's carefully indexed those against things like the Oxford English edition.
All publications are indexed by subject and title.
Capital gains would also be indexed for inflation--no more paying taxes on phony gains.
Officials now say that dollar deposits will be converted to pesos, though probably indexed to inflation.
